summary: Draft closed PR for menu main-rule decomp work. The diff implements mn_802307F8 fully, adds near-complete source for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714, updates related mnmain/mnmainrule prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures, removes stale asm placeholder comments for already-present mnmain helpers, and adds narrow dont_inline pragmas around two digit-buffer helpers. Bot output reports one new full match for mn_802307F8 and two 99.90% improvements for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source changes: implements mn_802307F8 and adds source bodies for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714; introduces temporary local GObj bitfield struct and externs for globals/floats used by the new bodies.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists mn_802307F8 as 100% matched and mn_8023164C/mn_80231714 as 99.90% improved

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for newly recovered mnmainrule functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mn_802307F8 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void mn_802307F8(s32, s32, s32)
raw_diff: mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 changed to void(void)

key_files.role: Adds dont_inline pragma blocks around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 and removes asm placeholder comments for mn_8022EA08, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022EB24, and mn_8022EB78.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/mn/mnmain.c hunks around mn_8022EA08/mn_8022EA78 and placeholder removals

key_files.role: Prototype cleanup for several mnmain functions from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void or typed signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mn_8022EBDC, mn_8022F268, mn_8022F360, and mn_8022F4CC prototype changes

key_files.role: Empty build-validation marker added in diff, but not listed in changed_files metadata.
evidence_refs: metadata_json.changed_files lists only four source/header files

what_changed: Implemented mn_802307F8 in src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c, including cleanup of an existing text object at data+0x130, selection of a text/index value from mn_804D4B96 or mn_803EC818, creation of an HSD_Text via HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, font_size initialization, and HSD_SisLib_803A6368 call.

Added source bodies for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 that update mn_804A04F0 menu flow state, choose hovered_selection/mode via switch on prev_menu, create a GObj with fn_8022F538, set render_plink through a local bitfield view, and call HSD_GObj_80390CD4(mn_80230E38(mode)); mn_80231714 additionally sets mn_804D6BD4 = 1.
Updated mnmainrule.h signatures for mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714 from unknown prototypes to concrete void-return prototypes.
Updated mnmain.h signatures for mn_8022EBDC, mn_8022F268, mn_8022F360, and mn_8022F4CC from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void or typed signatures.
Added scoped #pragma dont_inline on/off around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 in mnmain.c.
Removed asm-placeholder comments for mn_8022EA08, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022EB24, and mn_8022EB78 in mnmain.c.
Added an empty .build_validated file in the diff, although changed-file metadata only enumerates the four source/header files.
smart_moves: The PR keeps prototype updates with the recovered bodies instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the owning headers.
The PR uses ordinary switch control flow for the prev_menu dispatch in mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 rather than preserving a branch ladder in the submitted C.
The bot report gives narrow function-level progress for mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714 rather than only a broad build statement.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body proves a signature, update the owning header in the same slice; this PR changes mnmainrule and mnmain prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS alongside source recovery.

Function-level bot reports are useful intake evidence for matching status, but they should be recorded narrowly: here only mn_802307F8 is reported as 100%, while mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 are reported as 99.90% improvements rather than full matches.

Local bitfield overlay structs can express a recovered bitfield write, but in this PR the temporary GObjBitfield is introduced only in the C file and should be treated as a matching tactic or type-recovery bridge until stronger type evidence exists.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef GObjBitfield and ((GObjBitfield*) gobj)->render_plink assignment

naming_conventions.pattern: Address-style names retained for uncertain menu globals and functions, e.g. mn_804D6BD4, mn_804D4B96, mn_803EC818, mn_804DBE18 through mn_804DBE2C, mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: extern additions and function definitions in mnmainrule.c

naming_conventions.pattern: Short local role names are used where roles are apparent or register-derived: data, prev, gobj, mode, sis, zero, idx, r30.
Semantic field names are used where existing structs expose them, such as MenuFlow.prev_menu, cur_menu, hovered_selection and HSD_Text.font_size.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks were added around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on/off around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The PR slice does not include commentary explaining why the pragmas are required or adjacent-function regression evidence; under loaded standards this remains an exception tactic needing narrow evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Temporary local bitfield struct GObjBitfield is used to write gobj render_plink bits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Useful as a bridge for matching a bitfield write, but the slice does not prove this is the canonical HSD_GObj type representation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pointer-offset access on a u8* data base is used in mn_802307F8 for offsets 0x130 and 2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Grounded in the submitted diff, but type recovery may be incomplete; loaded standards prefer typed fields when recoverable.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch statement is used for prev_menu cases 0x0F and 0x10, with a default case assigning hovered_selection and mode.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Consistent with canonical control-flow cleanup, but no review discussion states it was chosen for source-style reasons.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Matched code increased by +248 bytes (+0.01%); mn_802307F8 in main/melee/mn/mnmainrule is reported as a new 100% match; mn_80231714 and mn_8023164C are reported as 99.90% improvements.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 to see whether the remaining 0.10% gaps were resolved or whether shared helper extraction was attempted.
Search for GObjBitfield or render_plink writes to determine whether a canonical HSD_GObj bitfield type already exists and whether this local typedef was later replaced.
Search for mn_802307F8 and offsets 0x130 or +2 in menu structs to identify a typed structure candidate for the data pointer.
Search for #pragma dont_inline around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 to see whether the pragma tactic persisted, was justified, or was removed.
Search later history for .build_validated handling in draft PRs, because this slice adds the marker but does not provide explicit validation details beyond the bot report.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state/title/author/dates and changed_files counts
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for source/header changes
text_corpus: empty PR body and one decomp-dev bot issue comment
raw_pr_json: merged_at null, draft true, state closed
notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments or reviews in the loaded slice.
The PR is closed with merged_at null and raw PR JSON indicates draft true, so this intake does not treat the changes as accepted upstream.

Bot report provides narrow function-level matching evidence, but no build log, objdiff details, or rationale for dont_inline pragmas, local bitfield struct, pointer-offset access, or extern additions.
Changed-file metadata lists four files, while the raw diff also adds an empty .build_validated file; this discrepancy is preserved rather than resolved by assumption.
The bot report supports a full match claim only for mn_802307F8. mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 are improvements at 99.90%, not accepted as matched functions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep header prototypes synchronized with recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2081 updates mnmainrule.h and mnmain.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures alongside decomp source changes; this is a concrete example of truthful header cleanup tied to body recovery.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnmainrule.h and mnmain.h prototype changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record partial matching evidence precisely
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report distinguishes mn_802307F8 as 100% matched from mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 as 99.90% improvements; curator records should preserve that distinction.

source_update_candidates.title: mnmainrule.c contains main-rule menu functions touched by PR 2081
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2081's diff shows src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c defining or working on menu main-rule functions including mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, mn_80231714, mn_802317E4, and references to MenuFlow mn_804A04F0.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-fact enrichment; should be curated against current source before acceptance.

source_update_candidates.title: Check whether local GObjBitfield should become a real type or be replaced
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2081 introduces a local GObjBitfield typedef solely to set render_plink on an HSD_GObj. Curator may want to flag this for source/type cleanup review if the pattern appears in current source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GObjBitfield typedef and render_plink assignment
source_update_candidates.reason: The slice supports this as a tactic, not as an accepted canonical type.

source_update_candidates.title: Audit dont_inline pragmas around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2081 adds scoped #pragma dont_inline around two mnmain digit-buffer helpers without loaded rationale beyond the bot report. If these pragmas remain, current-source curation should verify narrow matching/regression evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards treat pragmas as exceptional; PR evidence does not explain necessity.

rejection_notes: Do not promote mn_8023164C or mn_80231714 as matched; the only loaded verifier report says they are 99.90% improved.
Do not infer reviewer approval or merge acceptance; the PR has no review comments, is closed, and merged_at is null.
Do not treat the empty .build_validated file as proof of a successful build beyond the bot report present in the issue comment.
Do not canonize GObjBitfield, mn_804D4B96, mn_803EC818, or mn_804DBE18-through-mn_804DBE2C semantics from this slice alone; the diff only shows address-style externs and use sites.
Do not generalize that dont_inline pragmas are acceptable for mn helpers without separate verifier and regression evidence.
